caption: This instructional video presentation on "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" focuses on "Tutorial – 3" regarding the "Work/unit mass of gas liquefied." The slides feature a Temperature-Entropy (T-S) diagram depicting a thermodynamic cycle with various paths labeled "T=const," "s=const," and "p=const." As the video progresses, mathematical calculations are added to the screen. The values "W/m = 326" and "y = 0.1176" appear in blue boxes, followed by a final formula: "- W/m_l = W/y m = 326 / 0.1176 = 2772.1 J/g." The NPTEL logo is visible in the bottom left corner of each slide. At the bottom of the screen, text identifies the instructor and institution as "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay." The content remains static across the later frames once all the calculations have been revealed on the slide.
